description:
secretx-aws-sm/Cargo.toml:20 declares 'zeroize = { workspace = true }' but secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs contains zero references to zeroize, Zeroize, Zeroizing, or any zeroize-related symbol. cargo-machete would flag this as an unused dependency.

Two paths forward:

(a) Remove the dep. The crate's zeroization story is already documented in the lib.rs doc-comment (lines 5-13): the AWS SDK leaks the secret into a non-Zeroizing String before this crate sees it, so adding zeroize on top doesn't actually buy anything. The final SecretValue handles its own zeroize internally via secretx-core. There's no surface in this crate where 'use zeroize' would change behavior.

(b) Use it. If there's an unrealized intent (e.g. wrapping the put() Blob copy in a Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> before handing to the SDK — though the SDK then copies it again), implement that intent.

Recommendation: (a). Delete the line. Same pattern in secretx-aws-ssm/Cargo.toml:21 (also declared, also unused). secretx-aws-kms and secretx-azure-kv don't declare zeroize at all; secretx-gcp-sm declares and uses it.
title: secretx-aws-sm: zeroize declared as dependency but never used in source
